title: Make line plots
description: Practice creating line plots (dot plots) from data sets.

assessment_items.question_content:
The lengths of $4$ pencils are measured below.  

![](web+graphie:${ŌśŻ LOCALPATH}/images/07dc15dd4925eec02f5a693e91528027b9dc9584)

**Record the length of each pencil on the line plot.**  
*Click above the line plot to make dots for each measurement.*

[[Ōśā plotter 2]]
hints: First, let's find the length of each pencil.

All of the pencils begin at $0\text{ cm}$ on the ruler.  So, we can look at where our pencils end on the ruler to find their length.

![](web+graphie:${ŌśŻ LOCALPATH}/images/07dc15dd4925eec02f5a693e91528027b9dc9584)

$\redE{\text{Pencil A}}$ measure $\redE{8}$ cm long.

$\blueE{\text{Pencil B}}$ measure $\blueE{6}$ cm long.

$\goldE{\text{Pencil C}}$ measure $\goldE{5}$ cm long.

$\purpleD{\text{Pencil D}}$ measure $\purpleD{2}$ cm long.

Now, let's graph the measures on the line plot.

![](web+graphie:${ŌśŻ LOCALPATH}/images/58bf2f46b1b0eb137c627c24ef769ad235234dee)

assessment_items.question_content:
A park ranger counted stripes on each baby zebra.  She is going to make a line plot of the data.  

Zebra | Stripes
:-: | :-: | :-:
Majestic | $46$
Speed | $49$
Spot | $47$
Zooma | $47$
Rayas | $46$

**Graph the measurements from the table on the line plot.**   
*Click above the line plot to make dots for each of the measurements.*

[[Ōśā plotter 1]]
hints: Let's count how many Zebra's had $46$ stripes.

Two zebras had $\greenE{46}$ stripes.  

So, we place $2$ dots over $\greenE{46}$ on the number line.  

![](web+graphie:${ŌśŻ LOCALPATH}/images/fd03d6eb2999afce3fdee0cb87d6f184c454def8)
Now, let's graph the other measurements on the table.

Here is the completed line plot:

![](web+graphie:${ŌśŻ LOCALPATH}/images/3b4b099c36ce71a0c200184ff87775d5b56aabde)

assessment_items.question_content:
Randy is selling coupon books.  He's going to make a line plot of his sales data.  

Day | Coupon books sold
:-: | :-: | :-:
Monday | $17$
Tuesday | $15$
Wednesday | $17$
Thursday | $14$
Friday | $17$

**Graph the measurements from the table on the line plot.**   
*Click above the line plot to make dots for each of the measurements.*

[[Ōśā plotter 1]]
hints: Let's count how many days sold $14$ coupon books.

One weekday, Thursday, sold $\greenE{14}$ coupon books.

So, we place $1$ dot over $\greenE{14}$ on the number line.  

![](web+graphie:${ŌśŻ LOCALPATH}/images/6d9d7efd3f991b72aaca3c290cac8a6238774214)
Now, let's graph the other measurements on the table.

Here is the completed line plot:

![](web+graphie:${ŌśŻ LOCALPATH}/images/760c01f848e3a27c16b2aa14a991d8c8bda8fc6d)

assessment_items.question_content:
At a party, some friends measure the length of candy.
The friends are going to make a line plot of the data.  

Candy | Length ($\text{cm}$)
:-: | :-: | :-:
Chocolate bar | $29$
Licorice | $21$
Lollipop | $21$
Pack of gum | $21$
Sugar Dip | $25$
Minty bar | $27$
Snowball | $21$

**Graph the measurements from the table on the line plot.**   
*Click above the line plot to make dots for each of the measurements.*

[[Ōśā plotter 1]]
hints: Let's count how many candies measured $21\text{ cm}$.

The licorice, lollipop, pack of gum and snowball measured $\greenE{21}\text{ cm}$

So, we place $4$ dots over $\greenE{21}$ on the number line.  

![](web+graphie:${ŌśŻ LOCALPATH}/images/623c80b36d4d5c88c9fc3c654cb1c6117018a705)
Now, let's graph the other measurements on the table.

Here is the completed line plot:

![](web+graphie:${ŌśŻ LOCALPATH}/images/43c60b0a0ede75531b486b802b3bf058d438fb0e)

assessment_items.question_content:
The students in Ms. Carbon's second grade class each measured their plant's growth this week.   The students are going to make a line plot of the data.  

Student | Plant height ($\text{cm}$)
:-: | :-: | :-:
Louise | $7$
Francis | $5$
Amanda | $7$
Giancarlo | $5$
Annabeth | $8$

**Graph the measurements from the table on the line plot.**   
*Click above the line plot to make dots for each of the measurements.*

[[Ōśā plotter 1]]
hints: Let's count how many plants had a height of $5\text{ cm}$.

Francis and Giancarlo have plants that grew $5\text{ cm}$.

So, we place $2$ dots over $\greenE{5}$ on the number line.  

![](web+graphie:${ŌśŻ LOCALPATH}/images/573090139f915ae551ad674e50ec41ac3b3c7343)
Now, let's graph the other measurements on the table.

Here is the completed line plot:

![](web+graphie:${ŌśŻ LOCALPATH}/images/9b22fc683ed435e4414fc4c010ea5211c410bf11)

assessment_items.question_content:

Aurora | $97$
Pedro | $96$
Tony | $99$

**Graph the measurements from the table on the line plot.**   
*Click above the line plot to make dots for each of the measurements.*

[[Ōśā plotter 1]]
hints: Let's count how many toddlers ran $96\text{ m}$.

Pedro ran $96\text{ m}$.

So, we place $1$ dot over $\greenE{96}$ on the number line.

Now, let's graph the other measurements on the table.
